claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You do not live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 2 children:
  - Alex is 6 years old and does not live with you.
  - Blake is 16 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05)
  - Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Regarding Blake:
  - Claimant does not live in the UK
  - Child is 16 and in approved education
  - Claimant contributes £30.00/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant does not live in the UK; Child does not live with claimant and weekly upkeep (£20.00) is below the Child Benefit rate (£26.05); Child in local authority care for 9 weeks (>8) and does not spend 24+ hours/week at home

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you find out if you're eligible for Child Benefit.

To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you a few questions about your circumstances.

Let's start with the basics:

**How many children are you looking to claim Child Benefit for, and what are their names and ages?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! I'm looking to claim Child Benefit for my two children. I have Alex, who's 6 years old, and Blake, who's 16.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! It's great to meet you, and I'll help assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex (age 6) and Blake (age 16).

Let me ask a few more questions to understand your situation better.

**Do both Alex and Blake live with you?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, neither of them lives with me at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. Since neither child currently lives with you, I'll need to understand their situations a bit better.

**Could you tell me where Alex is currently living?** For example, are they living with another family member, in local authority care, in hospital, or somewhere else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is in local authority care at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for explaining Alex's situation. I understand that must be difficult.

**And where is Blake currently living?** Are they with another family member, in education, in care, or somewhere else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Blake is living with their other parent.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying.

Regarding Alex being in local authority care, I have a follow-up question:

**How long has Alex been in local authority care?** Has it been more than 8 weeks, or less than 8 weeks?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been a couple of months now, so more than 8 weeks.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. I now have a clearer picture regarding Alex.

For Alex in local authority care for more than 8 weeks, there may still be exceptions that could allow you to continue receiving Child Benefit. 

**Does Alex spend at least 24 hours a week at your home?** (For example, regular overnight visits or day visits that add up to at least 24 hours weekly)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex doesn't come home to stay with me. They're at the care home full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for clarifying that about Alex.

Now let me ask about Blake, who is living with their other parent.

**Are you currently contributing towards Blake's upkeep?** This could include things like clothes, presents, food, pocket money, or financial contributions towards their living costs. It could also be maintenance payments under a court order or agreement.

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards Blake's upkeep. I help with things like clothes and school supplies - I send money regularly for that.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful to know.

**Is the amount you contribute towards Blake's upkeep at least equal to the Child Benefit payment amount?** (For reference, the current Child Benefit rate is approximately £25.60 per week for the eldest or only child, and £16.95 per week for additional children.)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I send £30 a week, so it's more than the Child Benefit amount.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for confirming that.

One more important question about Blake:

**Is Blake's other parent (or anyone else) currently claiming Child Benefit for Blake?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, their other parent isn't claiming it. Nobody else is getting Child Benefit for Blake.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. Since Blake is 16 years old, I have one more question:

**Is Blake currently in approved education or training?** (For example, studying for A-levels, GCSEs, T Levels, or other qualifications at school or college, or on an approved training course)

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Blake is in sixth form doing A-levels.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for both Alex and Blake.

Let me summarise what you've told me and provide my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on all the information you've provided, here is my assessment of your Child Benefit eligibility:

---

**Alex (age 6) – Not Eligible**

Unfortunately, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. Since Alex has been in local authority care for more than 8 weeks and does not spend at least 24 hours a week at your home, the rules mean that Child Benefit entitlement stops. If Alex's situation changes in the future (for example, if they start spending at least 2 nights in a row each week or 7 or more days in a row at home), you may be able to claim again.

---

**Blake (age 16) – Eligible**

Good news! You are eligible to claim Child Benefit for Blake. Even though Blake lives with their other parent, you qualify because:
- You are contributing £30 per week towards Blake's upkeep, which is more than the Child Benefit amount
- Blake is in approved education (studying A-levels at sixth form)
- No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Blake

You can continue to receive Child Benefit for Blake while they remain in approved education or training, up until age 20.

---

I hope this helps clarify your situation. If you have any other questions, please feel free to ask.

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old and has been in local authority care for more than 8 weeks. Under the rules, Child Benefit usually stops after a child has been in care for more than 8 weeks. There is an exception if the child spends at least 24 hours a week at home, but you've confirmed that Alex stays at the care home full-time and does not come home to stay with you. Therefore, you are not eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.

Blake is 16 years old and living with their other parent. Although Blake doesn't live with you, you are contributing £30 per week towards Blake's upkeep (for clothes and school supplies), which is more than the Child Benefit payment amount. Under the rules, you can qualify for Child Benefit if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit towards looking after a child, even if they live with someone else. Additionally, Blake is in approved education (A-levels at sixth form), which means eligibility continues for children aged 16-19 in approved education. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant has two children: Alex (age 6) who is in local authority care full-time for more than 8 weeks, and Blake (age 16) who lives with their other parent but receives regular financial contributions from the claimant. For Alex, the care situation exceeds the 8-week limit with no applicable exceptions (child doesn't spend 24 hours a week at home). For Blake, the claimant meets the eligibility criteria by contributing more than the Child Benefit amount towards Blake's upkeep, Blake is in approved education, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake.

Additionally, Blake is in approved education (A-levels at sixth form), which means eligibility continues for children aged 16-19 in approved education. No one else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Blake. Therefore, you are eligible for Child Benefit for Blake.

For Alex, the care situation exceeds the 8-week limit with no applicable exceptions (child doesn't spend 24 hours a week at home). For Blake, the claimant meets the eligibility criteria by contributing more than the Child Benefit amount towards Blake's upkeep, Blake is in approved education, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for Blake.
